resumeLength: One page with finance logic and technical implementation both visible.

formattingRules.fontRecommendation: Technical-business hybrid layout with clean skills grouping.
formattingRules.bulletStyle: Pair financial object with technical method and measurable or bounded output.

writingRules.tone: Technical, financially precise, product-aware, and evidence-driven.
writingRules.bulletFormula: Action + financial product/data/model + technical method + risk, efficiency, accuracy, or user/business value
quantificationRules: Prefer concrete scope: sample size, stakeholder count, case volume, event size, model accuracy, project duration, budget, risk, or response time when supported by source facts.
When exact numbers are absent, use bounded evidence such as tool, method, deliverable, audience, or process scope; never invent metrics.
Use metrics differently by domain: technical performance for engineering, compliance/accuracy for audit, writing/research output for law and policy, service reliability for hospitality.

exampleBullets.strong: Built a portfolio-tracking prototype that integrated transaction data and visualized asset allocation for user review.
exampleBullets.reason: Combines product, data, and finance context in one clear bullet.

exampleBullets.strong: Automated Python data cleaning for market-price records, reducing manual preparation before risk analysis.
exampleBullets.reason: Shows tool, data object, workflow, and downstream finance use.

scoringCriteria.description: Conventional headings, readable layout, aligned dates, consistent spacing, and parser-friendly text.
Prominent sections and bullets match the target role and market context.
Bullets show action, method, scope, and outcome without unsupported claims.
Specific, concise, role-native language with no inflated AI phrasing.
Structure and density match the selected role, country, and CV/resume style.

promptSnippets.title: Financial Technology Resume routing rule
promptSnippets.content: When the target role matches Financial Technology / FinTech, use Financial Technology Resume as the primary industry route and adopt this formula: Action + financial product/data/model + technical method + risk, efficiency, accuracy, or user/business value.

promptSnippets.title: Financial Technology Resume language guardrail
promptSnippets.content: Preserve domain-native evidence for Financial Technology / FinTech; do not convert the profile into a generic tech, finance, or consulting resume unless the user target explicitly requires it.

sourceReferences.label: Local 123456 CV sample corpus
sourceReferences.note: 56 newly added CV/resume samples from docs/sample-resume-knowledge; abstracted into rules only, without copying resume text.

sourceReferences.label: Existing local resume-learning corpus
sourceReferences.note: Previously extracted school resume guides, templates, sample resumes, and industry profiles.
